reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: Expressed confusion over the patch's explanation of IP expansion, suggesting a need for clearer documentation or explanation.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, Jul 03, 2026 at 09:58:58PM -0400, Gregory Price wrote:
> +config SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
> +	bool "Syscall User Dispatch"
> +	depends on GENERIC_ENTRY
> +	default y
> +	help
> +	  Syscall User Dispatch lets a thread have its own system calls outside
> +          an allowed IP address range to be intercepted and redirected to a
> +          userspace signal handler.

I was very confused when I read this.  IP expands to Internet Protocol
long before it gets to Instruction Pointer in my brain.

2026-07-04.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new configurable feature, syscall_user_dispatch, which allows users to enable or disable the feature at both build and runtime. The feature is currently compiled in by default for all architectures that support CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL, but this patch makes it optional.
